QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_58

question.text: What role do clearing banks play in commodity settlement?

answer.text: They effect debit and credit instructions for margin collection and settlement fund transfers between clearing members and the CC
They trade commodity futures on behalf of clients
They set the daily settlement prices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Clearing banks are designated by exchanges to handle fund transfers — debiting members for margin/pay-in and crediting them for pay-out. Each member has settlement and client accounts at clearing banks. **The Trap:** Banks don't trade futures (unless they're also TMs). They don't set settlement prices. They don't operate warehouses. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII covers clearing bank functions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clearing bank efficiency directly impacts settlement speed — ensure your clearing member works with well-networked clearing banks for seamless fund transfers.
